content:
❌ Rejected the contribution.
Comment: The creator of this puzzle did not acknowledge User123 and 5DN1L's suggestions. I will withdraw this rejection once the creator addresses all the issues.
It would be better if you have extend restrictions with bigger values (because you use String (number with "?" mark) - its okay if input number will be ≤ 10^500)

[Goal]

- Please format the question mark as a constant: {{?}}

[Input / Output]

- Please follow CG's formatting guide: https://www.codingame.com/playgrounds/40701/help-center/statement. An example input statement could be: "<<Line 1>>: A number with one of its digits replaced with {{?}}."

[Constraints]

- Please specify the range of potential integers for the inputs of the test cases; e.g., "1 ≤ number < 10000".

[Stub Generator]

- Please change "100" to something like "5" to better reflect the range of potential digits of the numbers in your test cases.

[Cases]

- Consider whether you want to allow certain edge cases where either
1) there are multiple solutions (e.g., "?", "23?4", etc.)
2) the solution can have leading zero(s) (e.g., "?009")

- Please include more test cases. Ideally, each digit from 0-9 should appear as a solution to at least one test case, but a good spread of digits will suffice.

Thank you!
I does not get explained here, but a number is divisible by 9 exactly if the summ of its digits is divisible by 9.
I think it would be better, if you output the complete number, that way you got a little bit more String manipulation.

I generally agree with all of @User123's comments. Just adding two points:

Constraints / Cases
* I'm not sure why the cases cover 4-digit numbers only. A greater variety would be better. However, in case only 4-digit inputs are used, the constraint should become 1000 ≤ [[n]] ≤ 9999.

* Each test and its validator should be DIFFERENT while covering the same scenario.

Thanks.

This is my first time making the challange so I know there are many flaws. But the goal here is the input will be provided in string including ? so you have to find the number that fits in ? such that the number will be divisable by 9.

The 4-character limitation problem makes it possible to solve this problem in principle without loops and hard-code it (with conditions and checking of elements [0],[1],[2],[3]

Also, it's possible to enumerate all four-digit multiples of 9 for which three out of four digits match the string. This would essentially bypass the topic addressed in this problem (namely, the property of multiples of 9).

To prevent such decisions (this shouldn't be encouraged even at an easy level), I recommend adding large numbers to the tests, where there will be more than 4 numbers (or at least a variable number - 5, 6, 9-digit numbers with one "?" in the number)
